message:
🐛 Fix 3 inaccuracies found during verification (#314)

- KubeClipper: Remove invalid KC_REGION=en flag (not documented, use plain bash -)
- Keylime: Remove fabricated cert-manager prerequisite (not required by attestation-operator)
- Tokenetes: Restore correct tokenetes- prefix on filenames (tokenetes-deployment.yaml etc.)

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

files.patch:
@@ -14,10 +14,6 @@
         "title": "Clone the attestation-operator repository",
         "description": "Clone the Keylime attestation-operator repository which contains the Helm charts and manifests for deploying Keylime on Kubernetes:
```bash
git clone https://github.com/keylime/attestation-operator.git
cd attestation-operator
```"
       },
-      {
-        "title": "Install cert-manager (prerequisite)",
-        "description": "The attestation operator requires cert-manager for TLS certificate management:
```bash
kubectl apply -f https://github.com/cert-manager/cert-manager/releases/latest/download/cert-manager.yaml
kubectl wait --for=condition=Available --timeout=120s deployment/cert-manager -n cert-manager
kubectl wait --for=condition=Available --timeout=120s deployment/cert-manager-webhook -n cert-manager
```"
-      },
       {
         "title": "Deploy the Keylime Attestation Operator",
         "description": "Use the Makefile target to deploy the operator and Keylime components via Helm:
```bash
make helm-keylime-deploy
```
This installs the operator CRDs, the operator itself, and creates a default Keylime CR that deploys the registrar, verifier, and agent components.

Alternatively, install step by step:
```bash
make install           # Install CRDs
make deploy            # Deploy the operator
kubectl apply -f config/samples/  # Create Keylime CR
```"
@@ -58,8 +54,8 @@
         "description": "Keylime agents require TPM 2.0 hardware on worker nodes. If agent pods fail to start:
1. Verify TPM device exists: `ls /dev/tpm*` on the node
2. Check agent logs: `kubectl logs -n keylime -l app=keylime-agent`
3. On Kubernetes 1.26+, unprivileged TPM access may be available; on older versions, the agent may need privileged access"
       },
       {
-        "title": "cert-manager not ready",
-        "description": "If the operator fails to start with certificate errors, ensure cert-manager is fully ready:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n cert-manager
kubectl wait --for=condition=Available deployment/cert-manager-webhook -n cert-manager --timeout=120s
```"
+        "title": "Operator pod not starting",
+        "description": "If the operator pod fails to start, check the operator logs for errors:
```bash
kubectl logs -n keylime -l control-plane=controller-manager
```
Ensure the CRDs were installed with `make install` before deploying the operator."
       },
       {
         "title": "CRD not found errors",
@@ -110,7 +106,7 @@
       "make",
       "helm"
     ],
-    "description": "Kubernetes 1.24+ cluster with worker nodes that have TPM 2.0 hardware. cert-manager must be installed. git, make, helm, and kubectl must be available on your workstation."
+    "description": "Kubernetes 1.24+ cluster with worker nodes that have TPM 2.0 hardware. git, make, helm, and kubectl must be available on your workstation. For unprivileged agent pods, Kubernetes 1.26+ and the k8s-tpm-device-plugin are recommended."
   },
   "security": {
     "scannedAt": "2026-03-06T00:00:00.000Z",

@@ -12,7 +12,7 @@
     "steps": [
       {
         "title": "Install kcctl CLI",
-        "description": "Download and install the kcctl command-line tool on your management machine:\n```bash\ncurl -sfL https://oss.kubeclipper.io/get-kubeclipper.sh | KC_REGION=en bash -\n```\nVerify the installation:\n```bash\nkcctl version\n```"
+        "description": "Download and install the kcctl command-line tool on your management machine:\n```bash\ncurl -sfL https://oss.kubeclipper.io/get-kubeclipper.sh | bash -\n```\nVerify the installation:\n```bash\nkcctl version\n```"
       },
       {
         "title": "Deploy KubeClipper (All-in-One)",
@@ -30,7 +30,7 @@
     "resolution": {
       "summary": "A successful installation will show all KubeClipper components running via `kcctl get component`. The web console will be accessible at http://<server-ip> with the default admin credentials. You can then use the console or kcctl CLI to create and manage Kubernetes clusters.",
       "codeSnippets": [
-        "curl -sfL https://oss.kubeclipper.io/get-kubeclipper.sh | KC_REGION=en bash -",
+        "curl -sfL https://oss.kubeclipper.io/get-kubeclipper.sh | bash -",
         "kcctl deploy",
         "kcctl get node\nkcctl get component"
       ]
@@ -44,7 +44,7 @@
     "upgrade": [
       {
         "title": "Upgrade KubeClipper",
-        "description": "Download the latest kcctl CLI and run the upgrade command:\n```bash\ncurl -sfL https://oss.kubeclipper.io/get-kubeclipper.sh | KC_REGION=en bash -\nkcctl upgrade\n```"
+        "description": "Download the latest kcctl CLI and run the upgrade command:\n```bash\ncurl -sfL https://oss.kubeclipper.io/get-kubeclipper.sh | bash -\nkcctl upgrade\n```"
       }
     ],
     "troubleshooting": [

@@ -20,11 +20,11 @@
       },
       {
         "title": "Configure the Tokenetes manifests",
-        "description": "Before deploying, update the Kubernetes manifest files in the `kubernetes/` directory with your environment-specific values:

1. Replace `[your-namespace]` with your target namespace in all YAML files
2. Replace `[your-trust-domain]` with your SPIRE trust domain
3. Update the SPIRE agent socket path in `deployment.yaml` if your SPIRE agent socket is not at the default location (`/run/spire/sockets`)

```bash
# Create the target namespace
kubectl create namespace <your-namespace>
```"
+        "description": "Before deploying, update the Kubernetes manifest files in the `kubernetes/` directory with your environment-specific values:

1. Replace `[your-namespace]` with your target namespace in all YAML files
2. Replace `[your-trust-domain]` with your SPIRE trust domain
3. Update the SPIRE agent socket path in `tokenetes-deployment.yaml` if your SPIRE agent socket is not at the default location (`/run/spire/sockets`)

```bash
# Create the target namespace
kubectl create namespace <your-namespace>
```"
       },
       {
         "title": "Deploy the Tokenetes service",
-        "description": "Apply the Kubernetes manifests from the `kubernetes/` directory in order:
```bash
kubectl apply -f kubernetes/service-account.yaml
kubectl apply -f kubernetes/deployment.yaml
kubectl apply -f kubernetes/service.yaml
```"
+        "description": "Apply the Kubernetes manifests from the `kubernetes/` directory in order:
```bash
kubectl apply -f kubernetes/tokenetes-service-account.yaml
kubectl apply -f kubernetes/tokenetes-deployment.yaml
kubectl apply -f kubernetes/tokenetes-service.yaml
```"
       },
       {
         "title": "Verify the installation",
@@ -39,15 +39,15 @@
       "summary": "A successful installation will show tconfigd running in the tokenetes-system namespace and Tokenetes service pods running in your application namespace. You can verify TraT configurations with `kubectl get trats -n <your-namespace>`.",
       "codeSnippets": [
         "git clone https://github.com/tokenetes/tokenetes.git",
-        "kubectl apply -f kubernetes/service-account.yaml
kubectl apply -f kubernetes/deployment.yaml
kubectl apply -f kubernetes/service.yaml",
+        "kubectl apply -f kubernetes/tokenetes-service-account.yaml
kubectl apply -f kubernetes/tokenetes-deployment.yaml
kubectl apply -f kubernetes/tokenetes-service.yaml",
         "kubectl get pod -n tokenetes-system
kubectl get pod -n <your-namespace> | grep tokenetes",
         "kubectl get trats -n <your-namespace>"
       ]
     },
     "uninstall": [
       {
         "title": "Delete Tokenetes resources",
-        "description": "Remove the Tokenetes deployment, service, and service account from your namespace:
```bash
kubectl delete -f kubernetes/service.yaml
kubectl delete -f kubernetes/deployment.yaml
kubectl delete -f kubernetes/service-account.yaml
```"
+        "description": "Remove the Tokenetes deployment, service, and service account from your namespace:
```bash
kubectl delete -f kubernetes/tokenetes-service.yaml
kubectl delete -f kubernetes/tokenetes-deployment.yaml
kubectl delete -f kubernetes/tokenetes-service-account.yaml
```"
       },
       {
         "title": "Clean up CRDs and TraT resources",
@@ -65,7 +65,7 @@

```bash
kubectl apply -f kubernetes/service-account.yaml
kubectl apply -f kubernetes/deployment.yaml
kubectl apply -f kubernetes/service.yaml

@@ -83,7 +83,7 @@
       },
       {
         "title": "SPIRE agent socket not found",
-        "description": "If logs show errors about the SPIRE agent socket, verify the hostPath in the deployment matches your SPIRE agent socket location:
```bash
kubectl describe deployment tokenetes -n <your-namespace>
```
The default path is `/run/spire/sockets`. Update the `spire-agent-socket` volume's `hostPath.path` in `deployment.yaml` if your setup differs."
+        "description": "If logs show errors about the SPIRE agent socket, verify the hostPath in the deployment matches your SPIRE agent socket location:
```bash
kubectl describe deployment tokenetes -n <your-namespace>
```
The default path is `/run/spire/sockets`. Update the `spire-agent-socket` volume's `hostPath.path` in `tokenetes-deployment.yaml` if your setup differs."
       },

         "title": "TraT resources not created",
